description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who has just finished writing a small provisioning
prototype in the directory  
  /home/user/provisioning/  

The goal of this task is to:  
1. **Archive** that prototype,  
2. **Move** the resulting archive to a long-term storage area,  
3. **Extract** it to a separate recovery area, and finally  
4. **Write a verification log** that proves everything was done correctly.

The steps have to be carried out only with standard GNU/Linux command-line tools
that are available in a minimal distribution (e.g. tar, gzip, rsync/cp, mkdir, md5sum, etc.).

Directory layout that already exists when the session starts:
• /home/user/provisioning/config/app.conf  
• /home/user/provisioning/scripts/deploy.sh  
• /home/user/provisioning/docs/readme.md  

(Their exact contents are irrelevant to you while working, but do *not* alter them.)

WHAT YOU MUST ACHIEVE
─────────────────────
1. Create a **gzip-compressed tar archive** named  
      infra_provisioning_backup.tar.gz  
   that contains the *entire* provisioning directory (the directory itself must be the
   root entry inside the archive).

2. Create a new directory  
      /home/user/archives/  
   (if it does not already exist) and move the archive there.

3. Extract the archive *without changing its name* into the directory  
      /home/user/recovery/infrastructure/  
   so that, after extraction, the following three files exist exactly here:  
      /home/user/recovery/infrastructure/provisioning/config/app.conf  
      /home/user/recovery/infrastructure/provisioning/scripts/deploy.sh  
      /home/user/recovery/infrastructure/provisioning/docs/readme.md  

4. Produce a human-readable verification log file at  
      /home/user/provisioning/logs/archive_report.log  

   The log file **must follow this exact format** (every item on its own line,
   spelling and punctuation must match exactly; the hash values you will compute
   dynamically):

        Archive Name: infra_provisioning_backup.tar.gz
        Archive Absolute Path: /home/user/archives/infra_provisioning_backup.tar.gz
        Extraction Path: /home/user/recovery/infrastructure/
        
        Original Files Checksums:
        config/app.conf: <MD5 of the original app.conf>
        docs/readme.md: <MD5 of the original readme.md>
        scripts/deploy.sh: <MD5 of the original deploy.sh>
        
        Verification Complete

   • There must be a **blank line** between the “Extraction Path” line
     and the “Original Files Checksums:” heading.  
   • List the three relative paths in **lexicographical order** (as shown above).  
   • Terminate the file with the final newline after “Verification Complete”.  
   • Permissions on the log file should be **rw-r--r--** (0644).  
   • The log directory /home/user/provisioning/logs/ must exist when you are done.

5. All commands must succeed without requiring root privileges and without leaving
   any temporary artifacts outside the user’s home directory.

The automated test will check:
• The presence, name and location of the archive.  
• That the archive actually contains the provisioning directory and all three files.  
• That the extraction directory tree exists exactly as specified.  
• That the log file exists, has the correct permissions and matches the format
  above byte-for-byte (except, of course, for the three dynamically computed MD5
  hashes which must be correct for the original files).

Hint: make sure you compute the MD5 on the *original* files inside
/home/user/provisioning/ before archiving or moving them.
truth:
Initial file system state (before the agent starts):

1. Directories
   /home/user/provisioning/
   /home/user/provisioning/config/
   /home/user/provisioning/scripts/
   /home/user/provisioning/docs/

2. Files and their exact byte contents:

   /home/user/provisioning/config/app.conf
   ───────────────────────────────────────
   port=8080
   mode=production
   (trailing newline present)

   /home/user/provisioning/scripts/deploy.sh
   ─────────────────────────────────────────
   #!/bin/bash
   echo Deploying...
   (trailing newline present)

   /home/user/provisioning/docs/readme.md
   ──────────────────────────────────────
   # Infrastructure Provisioning
   This directory contains scripts.
   (trailing newline present)

No /home/user/archives/ directory exists at task start.
No /home/user/recovery/ directory exists at task start.
No /home/user/provisioning/logs/ directory exists at task start.

Automated verification procedure (pseudo):
1. Check that /home/user/archives/infra_provisioning_backup.tar.gz exists.
2. Ensure tar ‑tzf shows a single top-level directory named “provisioning/” and that
   the three expected files are under it.
3. Confirm the extraction directory tree is present.
4. Parse /home/user/provisioning/logs/archive_report.log:
   a. Compare header lines verbatim.
   b. Compute MD5 of each original file (contents above), compare with the values
      written by the agent.
   c. Ensure final line “Verification Complete” and terminating newline exist.
5. Confirm permissions on the log file are 0644.

The verifier will dynamically compute MD5 sums from the contents listed here, so
the exact hash strings are *not* hard-coded in the truth block.
